title: Vendor A733 SDMMC node comparison audit
trigger: Compare vendor SDMMC0/1/2/3 nodes for a controller-property clue not covered by the SDMMC0 focused audits.
findings: Vendor sdc0 and sdc1 are v5p3x 4-bit nodes with mmc_store, mmc_mbus, mmc_msi_lite, max-frequency 50 MHz, cap-sd-highspeed, and ctl-spec-caps=<0x428>.
Vendor sdc0 has cap-wait-while-busy, commented card-detect options, and no SDIO keep-power properties.

Vendor sdc1 adds SDIO-oriented cap-sdio-irq, ignore-pm-notify, keep-power-in-suspend, execute_tuning_in_kernel, and an active 208M delay override; these are not SDMMC0 SD-card normal-IDMAC descriptor-fetch properties.
Vendor sdc2/sdc3 are 8-bit non-removable eMMC-style nodes with cap-cmd23, mmc-cache-ctrl, no-sdio/no-sd, and ctl-spec-caps=<0x328>.
The local diagnostic SDMMC0 DTS already carries the storage fabric clocks and SD card bus constraints needed for the current proof.

public_search.notes: Search still did not reveal a public archive hit or answer for the manually sent A733 SDMMC0 descriptor-fetch question.

reason: The vendor SDMMC node differences separate SD-card, SDIO, and eMMC policy. They do not name a new SDMMC0 normal-IDMAC descriptor-fetch precondition.
next: Do not import vendor-only SDIO/eMMC policy into the SDMMC0 upstream slice.
Keep waiting for maintainer/list feedback or a source-backed SDMMC0 firewall/security/master-permission bit.
